spec_match.rationale: The editor demonstrates how a changing chip collection should rebalance, but native flex-wrap: balance cannot be observed because the installed Headless Chrome 149 build does not parse the value.

interactive.rationale: Wrap mode, balance mode, text/Enter chip creation, the add button, preset chips, and individually named removal buttons all update the collection and metrics after repair.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Adding and removing differently sized chips directly demonstrates the dynamic line-balancing use case described by the concept.

edge_cases.rationale: The page handles empty input, arbitrary labels, preset additions, removals, changing line counts, and unsupported balance builds with an explicit wrap fallback.

references.rationale: The parent feature links ChromeStatus feature 4547107962486784 and the CSS Flexbox Level 2 specification used by the conformance suite.

conformance.rationale: The feature route passes 3/5 assertions; only the balance parser and computed-style contracts fail because this browser build lacks the feature.

runtime_evidence.rationale: DevTools MCP reproduced the setMode ReferenceError and broken add button, then verified mode changes, typed and preset additions, removal, rebuilt metrics, and clean console output after repair.

telemetry_health.rationale: All post-repair interactions complete without runtime, console, resource, or assertion errors, and the unsupported branch is represented as page state.

design_system.rationale: The editor and compatibility notice use shared design tokens, and state is communicated through text and metrics rather than color alone.

accessibility.rationale: Mode controls expose pressed state and an unsupported description, metrics update in a polite status region, the add input is labelled, and each removal button now names its chip.

openQuestions.title: Verify native chip balancing in a supporting build
openQuestions.detail: Repeat add/remove interactions in a Chrome build where the two balance-specific conformance assertions pass, and capture how native balance changes per-line distribution versus wrap.

summary: The page mixed working event listeners with stale inline calls to IIFE-scoped functions, producing a setMode error and leaving the Add chip button dependent on an inaccessible addChip call. Inline handlers were removed, the add button was wired correctly, control state and dynamic metrics gained accessible semantics, removal buttons gained item-specific names, and unsupported browsers now receive a safe visible fallback.
